name: Dakota Community Bank & Trust, National Association
description_short: Community-focused bank operating in North Dakota and South Dakota since 1940, offering personal banking, business loans, mortgages, and wealth management services.
description_long:
Dakota Community Bank & Trust, National Association is a locally-owned bank headquartered in Bismarck, North Dakota that has served regional communities for over 80 years. Founded in 1940, the bank has grown alongside the communities it serves while maintaining its commitment to personalized, hometown banking service. The institution operates multiple branch locations across North Dakota and South Dakota, including Bismarck, Bowman, Dickinson, Glen Ullin, Hebron, Lincoln, Mandan, Mobridge, New Leipzig, Richardton, and Taylor.

The bank provides a comprehensive suite of financial services spanning personal and business banking. Retail offerings include checking and savings accounts, CDs with published rates, personal loans, home equity loans and lines of credit, and mortgage services with online application capabilities. Business customers can access agricultural loans, business operating loans, commercial insurance, and workplace retirement solutions including Simple IRAs and 401(k) plans. The bank also offers wealth management services through trusts and investment advisory services, along with insurance products covering farm and ranch, life, commercial, home, and auto coverage.

Dakota Community Bank & Trust emphasizes digital convenience while maintaining personalized service. They provide mobile banking, online bill pay, digital wallet integration, and secure online account access for both retail and business customers. The bank has invested in modern security features including chip and cyber security education, SecureAlerts, Positive Pay, and explicit scam prevention warnings. Their 80th anniversary milestone demonstrates longevity and community integration in a market where many regional banks have consolidated or closed.

As a full-service community bank, Dakota Community Bank & Trust serves customers seeking traditional banking relationships with local decision-making and personalized service. However, customers should note that detailed product terms, APRs, and fee structures are not fully transparent on the website, requiring direct contact for specific pricing information. The bank's strength lies in serving agricultural and small business communities in rural North Dakota and South Dakota rather than competing on digital innovation or product breadth with large national institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with published rates
Personal loans and home equity loans/lines of credit
Agricultural (farm) loans and business operating loans
Mortgage and home loan services with online application
Business banking including payroll and cash management services
Wealth management including trusts and investment advisory services
Insurance products (farm and ranch, commercial, life, home, auto)
Workplace retirement plans (Simple IRAs and 401(k) administration)
Mobile banking and online bill pay
Online account access with SecureAlerts and Positive Pay fraud prevention
pros: Established local institution with 80+ years of continuous operation in North Dakota and South Dakota
Comprehensive offerings across banking, lending, mortgages, insurance, and wealth management in one institution
Agricultural lending expertise with dedicated ag loan products and loan officers
Modern digital services including mobile banking, online bill pay, and secure online account access for business and retail customers
Published CD rates available to compare online, plus mortgage and home loan online application tools
Personalized service model emphasizing local decision-making and community involvement
Multiple branch locations across North Dakota and South Dakota for in-person banking access
cons: Limited geographic footprint (North Dakota and South Dakota only) compared to regional or national banks
Website does not display current APRs, fees, or detailed product terms—requires customer contact for pricing
No mention of digital banking innovation features like budgeting tools, robo-advisors, or API integration available at larger competitors
Smaller institution may have limited product customization compared to larger regional banks
No information on minimum account balances, account maintenance fees, or overdraft policies disclosed online
best_for: Agricultural and rural business owners in North Dakota and South Dakota seeking specialized farm lending and local relationships
Small to mid-size business owners seeking personalized commercial banking and retirement plan administration
Community residents in Bismarck, Mandan, Dickinson, and surrounding areas preferring local decision-making and face-to-face service
Customers seeking integrated financial services (banking, mortgages, insurance, wealth management) from a single trusted local institution
diagnosis: Best for agricultural businesses, small commercial enterprises, and community members in North Dakota and South Dakota seeking traditional local banking with personalized service and comprehensive financial products. Primary caveat: detailed pricing information (APRs, fees, minimums) is not available online, requiring direct contact with the bank, and the institution's geographic limitation and smaller scale means fewer digital innovation features compared to national competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
